info.name: Markdown README Viewer
info.summary: Render README.md files directly inside the Nextcloud Files view
info.description:
# Markdown README Viewer
Markdown README Viewer renders `README.md` files directly inside the Nextcloud Files app, similar to repository platforms such as GitLab.
## Current features
- Inline rendering of README files in the Files view - Support for common and hidden filename variants such as `README.md`, `Readme.md`, `.README.md`, `.Readme.md`, and `.readme` - Support for core Markdown elements such as: - headings - emphasis - code blocks - links - lists - Support for Markdown task lists (`- [ ]` / `- [x]`) with read-only, theme-aware checkbox markers - Automatic refresh when the file changes - Clean integration into the standard Files interface
## What's new in 1.0.3
- Added compatibility fallback registration for Nextcloud 32 file list headers - Fixed duplicate task list checkbox rendering for Markdown task lists - Stabilized task list rendering by styling plugin-generated checkbox markup directly - Cleaned up list spacing and minor CSS issues in the README header renderer
## Planned improvements
- Improved table rendering - Further Markdown compatibility improvements
## Use cases
This app is useful for:
- project folders with developer documentation - shared team directories - personal notes and structured folder documentation - repositories or synced workspaces stored in Nextcloud
## Notes
The app currently focuses on lightweight inline viewing of README files inside Nextcloud. Support for additional Markdown features is being improved over time.
info.author: Konstantin Unterweger
